canonical_use_cases: Semantic web search for AI applications and LLM pipelines
Research and information retrieval using neural/embeddings-based search
Finding similar content or competitive intelligence via URL-based similarity search
Building AI agents that require grounded, up-to-date web information
Searching specific content categories: research papers, news, tweets, company pages, personal sites
Deep research with structured JSON outputs using deep/deep-reasoning search types
Content retrieval with highlights, summaries, or full text for RAG workflows
Filtering search results by domain, date, category, or language
Real-time and low-latency search for autocomplete or live applications
Code context retrieval via the Context API over GitHub repos and Stack Overflow
required_parameters: query (string, required): the search query or natural language description of desired content
api_key (string, required): Exa API key from dashboard.exa.ai/api-keys, stored in EXA_API_KEY env var
numResults (integer, optional): number of results to return, default 10, max 100
contents (object, optional): controls what content is returned - text, highlights, summary
includeDomains/excludeDomains (array, optional): filter by domain, max 1200 entries each
startPublishedDate/endPublishedDate (string, optional): ISO date strings for date filtering
outputSchema (object, optional): JSON schema for structured output in deep/deep-reasoning search types
common_gotchas: numResults must be ≤100 when using highlights; exceeding this returns INVALID_NUM_RESULTS error
livecrawl and maxAgeHours are conflicting parameters - using both together causes INVALID_REQUEST (400)
company and people categories only support a limited set of filters; combining with unsupported filters may fail
additionalQueries parameter only works with type='deep' or type='deep-reasoning'; it is ignored for other types
outputSchema max nesting depth is 2 and max total properties is 10; more complex schemas degrade performance
Do not include citation or confidence fields in outputSchema - deep search returns grounding automatically and duplicating it increases latency/tokens
API key errors (401) mean the EXA_API_KEY environment variable is missing, expired, or invalid
402 errors indicate exhausted credits (NO_MORE_CREDITS) or exceeded budget (API_KEY_BUDGET_EXCEEDED)
Rate limit exceeded (429) requires exponential backoff retry logic
Content filtering options (verbosity, section filtering) require livecrawl='always' to function
best_practices: Use type='auto' by default for highest quality; switch to 'fast' or 'instant' only when latency is critical
Use highlights instead of full text in agentic workflows - reduces token usage by ~10x while preserving relevance
Store API key in EXA_API_KEY environment variable, never hardcode in source
Use category filters to dramatically improve result quality for specific content types (research paper, news, people, etc.)
Use maxAgeHours to control content freshness: 0=always livecrawl, -1=cache only, 24=prefer cache
For deep search structured outputs, use simple schemas (max depth 2, max 10 properties) for best performance
Use highlights with a specific 'query' sub-field when you want highlights optimized for a different query than the main search
Compare performance within latency classes (Fast vs Fast, not Fast vs Deep) when benchmarking
For RAG pipelines, prefer highlights mode for factual lookups and multi-step workflows
Implement exponential backoff for 429 rate limit errors; use request IDs when contacting support
anti_patterns: Using keyword search for complex conceptual queries where neural/semantic search would perform better
Requesting full text content when highlights would suffice, unnecessarily inflating token usage
Including citation or confidence fields in outputSchema for deep search (redundant and degrades performance)
Hardcoding API keys in source code instead of using environment variables
Using deep/deep-reasoning search types for simple factual queries where 'fast' or 'auto' would suffice at lower cost
Setting numResults above 100 when highlights are enabled
Using additionalQueries with non-deep search types expecting it to have effect
Mixing livecrawl and maxAgeHours parameters in the same request
Building complex deeply-nested outputSchema beyond 2 levels of nesting
Using instant search type for complex research queries requiring reasoning
dependencies: Python SDK: pip install exa-py (import from exa_py)
JavaScript SDK: npm install exa-js (import Exa from 'exa-js')
Optional: OpenAI SDK for building AI agents on top of Exa search results
